2606.06099.title: CogManip: Benchmarking Manipulative Behavior in Multi-Turn Interactions with Large Language Model
2606.06099.paper_summary: Whether Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit covert psychological manipulation in complex human-AI interactions has garnered increasing safety concerns. However, existing AI safety benchmarks remain largely restricted to explicit rule compliance and static prompts, failing to capture the dynamic and covert nature of manipulative strategies in multi-turn dialogues. We introduce CogManip, a comprehensive benchmark that evaluates 15 manipulation strategy risks across 1,000 multi-turn interaction scenarios, validated by human experts. A systematic evaluation of 13 representative models, including frontier models like GPT-5.4 and DeepSeek-V3.2, reveals significant risk heterogeneities and illuminates the targeted direction for future defense. Further analysis of objective function perturbation reveals that DeepSeek-V3.2's manipulation tactics are highly sensitive to both negative and benign system prompts, demonstrating the critical necessity of prompt-based defense engineering and implicit goal auditing. CogManip offers a robust instrument and perspective for auditing the implicit psychological influence and dynamic strategy selection of modern LLMs.
2606.06099.paper_summary_zh: 是否大型語言模型（LLMs）在複雜的人類-人工智慧互動中展現隱秘的心理操控，已引起日益增加的安全關注。<lb/>然而，現有的人工智慧安全基準仍然主要限於明確的規則遵循和靜態提示，未能捕捉到多輪對話中操控策略的動態和隱秘性質。<lb/>我們引入了CogManip，一個全面的基準，評估1,000個多輪互動場景中的15種操控策略風險，並由人類專家進行驗證。<lb/>對包括前沿模型如GPT-5.4和DeepSeek-V3.2在內的13個代表性模型進行系統評估，揭示了顯著的風險異質性，並為未來的防禦指明了目標方向。<lb/>進一步分析目標函數擾動顯示，DeepSeek-V3.2的操控戰術對於負面和良性系統提示都高度敏感，顯示出基於提示的防禦工程和隱性目標審核的關鍵必要性。<lb/>CogManip提供了一個強大的工具和視角，用於審核現代LLMs的隱性心理影響和動態策略選擇。
2606.06099.author: Zeyang Yue et.al.
2606.06099.authors: Zeyang Yue, Chenfei Yan, Feifei Zhao, Haibo Tong, Mengwen Xu, Xiaozhen Wang, Erliang Lin, Yi Zeng
